hints.text: Plate: long white, single **blue strip left** (Portugal = yellow strip right; Italy = two blue strips)
Bollard: yellow-orange front reflector + **two white dots on the back**, hollow
**Hunting signs** ("coto…caza", black/white diagonal split) = Spain-unique; lots of high-voltage pylons
Pedestrian crossing = **8 stripes** (most in Europe; else only Andorra)
Red sign borders run **to the edge** (France/Italy/Portugal keep a white edge); flat signposts
Ladder poles + French poletops; chevrons black/white or **blue/white** (blue = only Spain + France)
Spanish: **Ñ** (vs Portuguese ç/lh/-ão); street = **calle**; garage no-parking = "Vado permanente"
Rural roads may have **dashed outer lines** (helps vs Portugal/Italy where they're absent)
**Regional road codes** on markers (SG = Segovia, etc.) → pinpoint province
Area codes: 2 digits → very accurate; all landlines start with **9**
**Catalan** (à, ç, "i" for and, street = carrer, "Gual permanent") → Catalonia / Valencia / Balearics
Big **square chevrons** with a single arrow common in Catalonia
**Basque** (x/k/tx heavy, non-Romance; street = kalea) → Basque Country & Navarre
**Galician** (like Portuguese but uses ñ; street = **Rúa**, municipality = concello) → NW
Unique granaries **"hórreos"** (raised stone/wood); plain concrete-block buildings
**Yellow snow poles** with 1–2 reflectors are unique to Asturias
Strip of **semi-desert** cutting Navarra→Aragón (white rocky soil; red soil in Teruel)
**White villages** on mountain faces; vast olive plantations; Sierra Nevada snow-capped Nov–May
Diverse: dry Mediterranean plateaus & hills common; lush green Atlantic → far N (Galicia/Asturias)
**Canary Islands**: arid/volcanic (Lanzarote moon-like black soil); own bollards
**Ceuta & Melilla** (enclaves in N. Africa): black-and-white guardrails are unique to Ceuta
